title: Race-lap ranking specialist blended with beta-calibrated classifier logits
summary: Next-lap pit decisions are partly relative: on a given lap, only a few drivers are truly in-window.
rationale: Next-lap pit decisions are partly relative: on a given lap, only a few drivers are truly in-window. Learning-to-rank can exploit that within-lap competition structure, while beta calibration is a strong low-variance way to align skewed scores before blending.
implementation_hint: Train an XGBoost ranker with qid = Year+Race+LapNumber so each query is the active field on one lap, using the current best engineered features plus any sequence/state features. Convert ranker scores to probabilities with fold-wise beta calibration, then blend calibrated logits with the main binary classifier using only purged/grouped OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering among same-lap candidates and reduce noisy standalone probabilities when fused with the stronger tabular classifier.
risk: Ranking only helps if each lap has enough positives/near-positives to learn from; calibration or blending done on non-purged OOF can create fake gains.
